a.def: Used before a noun to refer to one thing (not specific)
ex: I have a dog.
She wants a new book.
He is a teacher.

abdicate.def: To give up a position of power or responsibility formally
ex: The king abdicated the throne after decades of rule.
She refused to abdicate her responsibility as team leader.

aberration.def: A departure from what is normal or expected
ex: The warm winter was dismissed as a statistical aberration.
His violent outburst was an aberration in an otherwise calm demeanor.

ability.def: The power or skill to do something
ex: She has the ability to learn languages quickly.
His ability to solve problems impressed everyone.
Tell me about your day.
There are about 50 people here.
What is this book about?

above.def: In a higher position than something
ex: The plane flew above the clouds.
The painting hangs above the door.

abroad.def: In or to a foreign country
ex: I've never been abroad before.
Many students go abroad for university.

abstain.def: To deliberately choose not to do or have something
ex: Three members abstained from voting on the proposal.
Doctors advised him to abstain from alcohol during treatment.

accentuate.def: To make something more noticeable or prominent
ex: The lighting accentuated the sharp angles of the architecture.
Economic inequality was accentuated by the pandemic.

accept.def: To agree to take something offered
ex: She accepted the job offer immediately.
I can't accept your apology this time.

accident.def: An unexpected event that causes damage or injury
ex: There was a car accident on the highway this morning.
She had an accident while riding her bicycle.

accolade.def: An award or privilege granted as a special honor
ex: The film received accolades from critics worldwide.
Winning the Nobel Prize is the highest accolade in science.

achieve.def: To succeed in doing something after trying hard
ex: He achieved his goal of running a marathon.
You can achieve anything if you work hard enough.

acquire.def: To get or gain something over time
ex: She acquired new skills during the training.
The company acquired a smaller firm last year.

across.def: From one side to the other side of something
ex: She walked across the street.
The bridge goes across the river.

act.def: To do something; to perform in a play or film
ex: We must act quickly.
She can act very well.
He decided to act on the advice.

action.def: The process of doing something to achieve a goal
ex: We need to take action now.
The movie is full of action.

activity.def: Something that you do for enjoyment or a purpose
ex: Swimming is a fun activity.
There are many activities for children here.

actually.def: Used to say what is really true
ex: I actually enjoyed the movie more than I expected.
He looks young, but he is actually 40 years old.

adapt.def: To change to fit new conditions
ex: It took me months to adapt to the new city.
Animals adapt to their environment over time.

add.def: To put something together with something else
ex: Please add sugar to my coffee.
Add these numbers together.
I want to add one more thing.

adept.def: Very skilled or proficient at something
ex: She is adept at navigating complex political situations.
The surgeon was adept with both traditional and robotic techniques.
The hotel is adjacent to the train station.
They bought the adjacent plot of land.

admire.def: To respect or look up to someone
ex: I really admire her dedication to her work.
He admires people who stand up for their beliefs.

admit.def: To agree that something is true, often unwillingly
ex: He admitted that he had made a mistake.
I must admit, the food was delicious.

adolescent.def: A young person in the process of developing from a child into an adult
ex: Adolescents often struggle with identity.
The program targets adolescents aged 13 to 17.

adopt.def: To take something as your own or accept a new idea
ex: They decided to adopt a child.
The company adopted a new strategy.

adult.def: A person who is fully grown
ex: This film is only for adults.
As an adult, you need to make your own decisions.

advantage.def: Something that helps you or is useful
ex: Speaking two languages is a big advantage.
Living near the office has many advantages.
Traveling alone was a great adventure.
The children love reading adventure stories.
She showed remarkable courage in the face of adversity.
The community grew stronger through shared adversity.

advice.def: An opinion about what someone should do
ex: Can you give me some advice about my job?
She followed her mother's advice and studied harder.

aesthetic.def: Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty
ex: The building has great aesthetic value.
She made the choice for purely aesthetic reasons.

affect.def: To have an influence on something
ex: The weather can affect your mood.
This decision will affect everyone in the team.

affiliate.def: An organization officially connected to a larger one
ex: The company has affiliates in over 30 countries.
Our local station is an affiliate of the national network.

affinity.def: A natural liking or attraction to something or someone
ex: He has always had an affinity for classical music.
The two cultures share a deep affinity in their storytelling traditions.

afford.def: To have enough money to buy something
ex: We cannot afford a new car right now.
Can you afford to eat out every day?

afraid.def: Feeling fear; worried that something bad may happen
ex: She is afraid of spiders.
Do not be afraid to ask questions.
We had coffee after dinner.
I will call you after work.
What happened after that?

afternoon.def: The time between noon and evening
ex: I will see you this afternoon.
She always sleeps in the afternoon.
Can you say that again?
I want to go there again.
She smiled again.

age.def: The number of years someone has lived
ex: What is your age?
Children of all ages can play.
She started working at age 18.

aggravate.def: To make a problem or situation worse
ex: Pollution can aggravate respiratory conditions.
His rude comments only aggravated the situation.

aggregate.def: A whole formed by combining several separate elements
ex: In the aggregate, consumer spending rose by 4% last year.
The team won the match on aggregate after two legs.
I moved here two years ago.
A long time ago, there was a king.

agree.def: To have the same opinion as someone
ex: I agree with you completely.
They could not agree on a plan.
Do you agree?
My aim is to finish the project by Friday.
The aim of the course is to improve your writing.

air.def: The invisible gas that we breathe
ex: Open the window for fresh air.
Birds fly through the air.
The air smells clean after rain.

airport.def: A place where planes take off and land
ex: We arrived at the airport early.
The airport is very busy today.

align.def: To arrange in a line or bring into agreement
ex: We need to align our goals with the company's vision.
The text should be aligned to the left margin.
The fish was still alive when we caught it.
He feels more alive when he is near the sea.

all.def: Every one of; the whole amount
ex: All students must attend.
She ate all the cake.
We are all here now.

allegation.def: A claim that someone has done something wrong, typically without proof
ex: The minister denied all allegations of corruption.
Serious allegations were made against the pharmaceutical company.

alleviate.def: To make suffering or a problem less severe
ex: The medicine helped alleviate her pain.
New policies aim to alleviate poverty in rural areas.
Smoking is not allowed in this building.
My parents allowed me to stay out late.

allude.def: To refer to something indirectly or briefly
ex: The author alluded to Greek mythology throughout the novel.
She alluded to problems at home but never gave details.
He also speaks French.
I also want to come.
She is also a good singer.
She always arrives on time.
I always drink water in the morning.
He is always happy.
The view from here is amazing.
She is an amazing cook.
What an amazing day!

ambiguity.def: The quality of being open to more than one interpretation
ex: The ambiguity of the contract led to a legal dispute.
Great literature often thrives on moral ambiguity.

ambition.def: A strong desire to achieve something
ex: Her ambition is to become a neurosurgeon.
He lacked the ambition to climb the corporate ladder.
Despite his fame, he remained amiable and approachable.
The amiable shopkeeper always remembered his customers' names.
The announcement came amid growing public concern.
She remained calm amid the chaos.

amount.def: How much of something there is
ex: A large amount of water was wasted.
What amount of sugar do you need for the cake?

ancient.def: Very old, from a long time ago
ex: We visited an ancient temple in Greece.
The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids.

anecdotal.def: Based on personal accounts rather than systematic evidence
ex: The evidence for the treatment's effectiveness is purely anecdotal.
Anecdotal reports suggest the problem is far more widespread.

angry.def: Feeling strong displeasure about something unfair
ex: He was angry about the decision.
Do not be angry with me.
She looked angry.

animal.def: A living creature that is not a plant or human
ex: A dog is a popular animal.
Wild animals live in the forest.
What is your favorite animal?
The company announced a new product today.
They announced their engagement at the party.
The annual conference takes place in March.
She receives an annual bonus from her company.
Would you like another cup of tea?
Let us try another way.
She moved to another city.

answer.def: Something you say or write in reply to a question
ex: What is the answer to this question?
She did not answer my call.
Please answer the door.

antagonist.def: A person who actively opposes or is hostile to someone
ex: The novel's antagonist is a corrupt government official.
He became the chief antagonist of the proposed reforms.

anxiety.def: A feeling of worry or nervousness
ex: She felt anxiety before the exam.
Many people suffer from anxiety about the future.
Do you have any questions?
I do not have any money.
Any student can join.

anyone.def: Any person; it does not matter who
ex: Is anyone home?
Anyone can learn to cook.
I did not see anyone there.

anything.def: Any thing; it does not matter what
ex: Is there anything I can do?
She can eat anything.
I do not want anything.

anyway.def: Used to change the subject or add something
ex: It was raining, but we went out anyway.
Anyway, let me tell you what happened next.

apartment.def: A set of rooms for living in, inside a building
ex: They live in a small apartment.
I am looking for a new apartment.

apathy.def: Lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
ex: Voter apathy resulted in the lowest turnout in decades.
Public apathy toward climate change frustrated the scientists.

apologize.def: To say you are sorry for something
ex: He apologized for being late.
You should apologize to her for what you said.

apparatus.def: Equipment or machinery needed for a particular activity or purpose
ex: The laboratory has the latest scientific apparatus.
The state apparatus was used to suppress dissent.
It was apparent that she was unhappy.
The reason for his absence became apparent later.

appeal.def: To be attractive or interesting to someone
ex: The idea of working from home appeals to me.
This kind of music doesn't appeal to everyone.

appetite.def: A natural desire to satisfy a bodily need, especially for food
ex: Stress can reduce your appetite.
There is a growing appetite for renewable energy.

apple.def: A round fruit that is red, green, or yellow
ex: I eat an apple every day.
She bought some apples from the market.

apply.def: To make a formal request for something
ex: I applied for three different jobs last week.
You should apply before the deadline.

appreciate.def: To be grateful for something or understand its value
ex: I really appreciate your help.
She doesn't appreciate how lucky she is.

approach.def: A way of dealing with something
ex: We need a different approach to this problem.
His approach to teaching is very creative.

approve.def: To officially accept or agree to something
ex: The manager approved my vacation request.
Her parents don't approve of her boyfriend.

arbitrary.def: Based on random choice rather than reason or system
ex: The decision seemed entirely arbitrary and unfair.
Arbitrary deadlines were imposed without consulting the team.
The arduous journey across the mountains took three weeks.
Completing a PhD is an arduous but rewarding process.

area.def: A part of a place, city, or country
ex: This area is very quiet.
We searched the whole area.
There is a park in this area.

argue.def: To disagree with someone, often angrily
ex: They always argue about money.
He argued that the plan was too expensive.

arm.def: The body part between your shoulder and your hand
ex: She broke her arm last week.
He put his arm around her.

around.def: On all sides of; in the area near
ex: There are trees around the house.
She looked around the room.
Is there a bank around here?
Can you arrange a meeting for tomorrow?
She arranged the flowers in a vase.

arrive.def: To reach a place, especially at the end of a journey
ex: We will arrive at 6 pm.
The train arrived on time.
When did you arrive?

art.def: Paintings, drawings, music, or other creative works
ex: She studies art at school.
This museum has beautiful art.
Art makes life more interesting.

articulate.def: To express an idea or feeling clearly in words
ex: She articulated her concerns at the meeting.
He struggled to articulate what he meant.

artist.def: A person who creates art, such as paintings or music
ex: He is a famous artist.
The artist painted this picture.

ask.def: To say something to get an answer or information
ex: Can I ask you a question?
She asked for help.
Ask him where he lives.

aspiration.def: A hope or ambition of achieving something
ex: Many young people have aspirations of working abroad.
The country's aspirations for EU membership grew stronger.

assertion.def: A confident and forceful statement of fact or belief
ex: His assertion that the earth is flat was widely ridiculed.
The paper's central assertion lacks supporting evidence.

assume.def: To think something is true without proof
ex: I assumed you knew about the meeting.
Don't assume anything until you have the facts.

at.def: Used to show a place, time, or direction
ex: I am at home now.
The meeting is at 3 pm.
Look at this!

attack.def: To try to hurt someone or something
ex: The dog attacked the stranger.
The army attacked the city at night.

attempt.def: An effort to do something difficult
ex: He made several attempts to pass the exam.
It was her first attempt at cooking a full meal.

attend.def: To go to an event or a place
ex: Will you attend the meeting tomorrow?
She attends a language course every evening.

attitude.def: The way you think or feel about something
ex: She has a very positive attitude toward life.
His attitude at work needs to improve.

attractive.def: Pleasant to look at or interesting
ex: She found him very attractive.
The city center is attractive to tourists.

attrition.def: Gradual reduction in strength or numbers through sustained pressure
ex: Staff numbers were reduced through natural attrition rather than layoffs.
The war of attrition exhausted both armies over four years.

audience.def: People who watch or listen to a show
ex: The audience clapped loudly after the concert.
The speaker asked the audience a question.

auspicious.def: Indicating a good chance of success; favorable
ex: The project got off to an auspicious start with strong funding.
It was not an auspicious moment to ask for a raise.

authentic.def: Genuine and not a copy; real
ex: The restaurant serves authentic Italian cuisine.
Is this an authentic Picasso painting?

authority.def: The power to give orders or make decisions
ex: The police have the authority to stop you.
She spoke with great authority on the subject.

autonomous.def: Having the freedom to govern itself or control its own affairs
ex: The region became an autonomous territory.
She is an autonomous thinker who questions everything.

averse.def: Having a strong dislike or opposition to something
ex: She is not averse to taking risks when necessary.
Many investors are averse to volatile markets.

avoid.def: To stay away from something or someone
ex: Try to avoid eating too much sugar.
He avoids driving in heavy traffic.
Are you aware of the risks involved?
I wasn't aware that the shop had closed.

away.def: To or at a distance from here
ex: She walked away quickly.
The beach is far away.
Go away!
The weather was awful all week.
I feel awful about forgetting her birthday.

baby.def: A very young child who cannot yet walk or talk
ex: The baby is sleeping.
She had a baby last month.
What a cute baby!

back.def: The part of your body behind you; returning
ex: My back hurts today.
She came back from work.
I will be back soon.

backdrop.def: The setting or background for a scene or event
ex: The mountains provided a stunning backdrop for the wedding.
The reforms took place against a backdrop of economic crisis.

background.def: A person's education, experience, or family history
ex: People from different backgrounds work here.
Can you tell me about your professional background?

bad.def: Not good; of low quality or harmful
ex: That was a bad idea.
The weather is bad today.
I feel bad about it.

bag.def: A container made of paper, plastic, or leather
ex: She put the books in her bag.
I need a shopping bag.

ball.def: A round object used in games and sports
ex: Throw the ball to me.
The children played with a ball.

ban.def: To officially say that something is not allowed
ex: The city banned smoking in all public parks.
He was banned from driving for a year.

bank.def: A place where you keep your money safely
ex: I need to go to the bank.
She works at a bank.
The bank closes at 5 pm.
You need to learn the basic rules first.
The hotel room was basic but clean.

basis.def: The main reason or starting point for something
ex: On what basis did you make that decision?
We meet on a weekly basis.

bath.def: Washing your whole body in water
ex: I take a bath every evening.
The baby loves bath time.

be.def: To exist; to have a quality or state
ex: I want to be a doctor.
She will be here soon.
To be or not to be.

beach.def: An area of sand next to the sea
ex: We went to the beach yesterday.
The beach was very crowded.
Let us build a sandcastle on the beach.

beat.def: To win against someone in a game or fight
ex: Our team beat them 3 to 1.
Nobody can beat her at chess.

beautiful.def: Very pleasing to look at or hear
ex: What a beautiful day!
She has a beautiful voice.
The garden is beautiful in spring.
I stayed home because I was sick.
She is happy because she passed the exam.

become.def: To start to be something; to grow into
ex: He wants to become a pilot.
It became very cold at night.
She became famous.

bed.def: A piece of furniture for sleeping on
ex: I go to bed at 10 pm.
The bed is very comfortable.
Time for bed!

before.def: Earlier in time; in front of
ex: Wash your hands before eating.
I have seen this movie before.
She arrived before me.
The movie will begin at 8 pm.
Let us begin the lesson.
It began to rain.

behalf.def: In the interest of or as a representative of someone
ex: I'm speaking on behalf of the whole team.
She accepted the award on his behalf.

behave.def: To act in a particular way
ex: The children behaved very well at dinner.
Why is he behaving so strangely today?
The garden is behind the house.
She hid behind the door.
I left my bag behind.

believe.def: To think that something is true or real
ex: I believe you.
She does not believe in ghosts.
I believe we can win.

belong.def: To be owned by or be a member of
ex: This book belongs to my sister.
He belongs to a local football club.

benchmark.def: A standard or point of reference against which things may be compared
ex: The report sets a benchmark for future studies.
Their product became the benchmark in the industry.
The benevolent donor funded scholarships for underprivileged students.
History rarely remembers rulers as entirely benevolent.
Fortunately, the tumor was benign.
He gave a benign smile to the nervous student.

best.def: Of the highest quality; better than all others
ex: She is my best friend.
This is the best pizza I have ever eaten.
Do your best!

better.def: Of a higher quality than something else
ex: This one is better than that one.
I feel better today.
She is a better cook than me.

between.def: In the space separating two things
ex: The park is between the school and the hospital.
Choose between these two.

big.def: Large in size, amount, or importance
ex: They live in a big house.
It was a big mistake.
She has big brown eyes.

bird.def: An animal with wings and feathers that can usually fly
ex: A bird is singing in the tree.
I saw a beautiful bird today.

bite.def: To cut something with your teeth
ex: The dog tried to bite the mailman.
She bit into the apple hungrily.

black.def: The darkest color, like the night sky
ex: She wore a black dress.
The cat is black.
I drink black coffee.

blame.def: To say someone is responsible for something bad
ex: Don't blame me for your mistakes.
They blamed the rain for the accident.

bland.def: Lacking strong features or characteristics; dull
ex: The food was bland and needed more seasoning.
His speech was bland and failed to inspire anyone.
The blind man used a stick to walk.
She has been blind since birth.

block.def: To stop something from moving or happening
ex: A fallen tree blocked the road.
He blocked the ball with his hands.

blue.def: The color of the sky on a clear day
ex: The sky is blue today.
She has blue eyes.
I bought a blue shirt.

blueprint.def: A detailed plan or design for something
ex: The architect showed us the blueprint for the new library.
The document serves as a blueprint for reform.

blunt.def: Speaking in a direct and honest way, even if it upsets people
ex: She was blunt about his poor performance.
To be blunt, I think your plan will fail.

body.def: The physical form of a person or animal
ex: Exercise is good for your body.
The human body needs water.
New evidence bolstered her argument.
The government took steps to bolster the economy.
The bomb was found before it exploded.
They dropped bombs on the city during the war.

bone.def: One of the hard parts inside your body
ex: She broke a bone in her arm.
The dog was chewing on a bone.

book.def: A set of pages with writing or pictures, inside a cover
ex: I am reading a good book.
She wrote a book about cooking.
This book has 200 pages.

boom.def: A period of rapid economic growth or increase
ex: The city experienced a construction boom in the 1990s.
The tech boom created thousands of new jobs.
We crossed the border into France.
The town is near the border with Germany.

born.def: Coming into life; starting to exist
ex: She was born in 1990.
I was born in Istanbul.
A star is born.

borrow.def: To take something that you will give back later
ex: Can I borrow your pen for a moment?
She borrowed three books from the library.

boss.def: The person in charge at work
ex: My boss gave me the day off.
She is a strict but fair boss.
Both children are happy.
I like both of them.
She speaks both English and French.
The keys were at the bottom of my bag.
There is a shop at the bottom of the hill.

boy.def: A male child or young man
ex: The boy is playing in the park.
He was a quiet boy.

brand.def: A type of product made by a particular company
ex: What brand of coffee do you prefer?
They launched a new clothing brand last year.
The brave firefighter saved the child.
You were very brave at the dentist today.

bread.def: A common food made from flour and water, baked in an oven
ex: I bought some bread from the bakery.
Would you like some bread?

break.def: To damage something so it separates into pieces; a rest
ex: Be careful not to break the glass.
Let us take a short break.
She broke her leg.

breakfast.def: The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning
ex: What did you have for breakfast?
I always eat breakfast at 8 am.

breath.def: The air that goes in and out of your lungs
ex: Take a deep breath and relax.
He was out of breath after running.

brevity.def: Concise and exact use of words; shortness of time
ex: The brevity of his speech surprised everyone.
For the sake of brevity, I will summarize only the key findings.

bridge.def: A structure built over a river or road so people can cross
ex: We walked across the old stone bridge.
The bridge connects the two sides of the city.

bright.def: Full of light or strong in color
ex: The sun was very bright this morning.
She wore a bright red dress to the party.

bring.def: To carry something or someone to a place
ex: Please bring your book to class.
She brought flowers for me.
Bring your friends!

brink.def: The extreme edge of land before a steep slope; a point very close to something
ex: The company was on the brink of bankruptcy.
Scientists believe we are on the brink of a major discovery.
The river is very broad at this point.
He has a broad knowledge of history.

brother.def: A male who has the same parents as you
ex: My brother is older than me.
She has two brothers.
He and his brother look alike.

brown.def: The color of wood, earth, or chocolate
ex: She has brown hair.
The dog is brown.
I like brown shoes.

budget.def: The amount of money available to spend
ex: We need to stay within our budget.
The project went over budget by 20 percent.

build.def: To make something by putting parts together
ex: They want to build a new house.
He built a table from wood.

burden.def: A heavy load or something that causes worry or difficulty
ex: Caring for elderly parents can be a financial burden.
She didn't want to be a burden on her family.

bureaucracy.def: A system of government with many complicated rules and processes
ex: Excessive bureaucracy slows down the approval process.
She spent weeks navigating the bureaucracy to obtain a visa.

bus.def: A large vehicle that carries many passengers
ex: I take the bus to work.
The bus arrives every 10 minutes.
She missed the bus.

business.def: The activity of buying and selling; a company
ex: He started his own business.
Business is good this year.
She studies business at university.

busy.def: Having a lot of things to do; not free
ex: I am very busy today.
The street is always busy.
She is too busy to come.

but.def: Used to introduce something different or opposite
ex: I want to go, but I am tired.
She is small but strong.
It is cheap but good.

buy.def: To get something by paying money for it
ex: I want to buy a new phone.
She buys groceries every Saturday.
Where did you buy this?

by.def: Near; through the action of; before a time
ex: Sit by the window.
This book was written by her.
I will finish by Friday.

calibrate.def: To adjust or mark a measuring instrument for accuracy
ex: The equipment must be calibrated before each experiment.
We need to calibrate our response to the level of threat.

call.def: To use a phone to speak to someone; to give a name
ex: I will call you tonight.
They call him Tom.
She called a taxi.
The sea was calm and beautiful.
Stay calm and don't panic.

camp.def: A place where people stay in tents
ex: The kids went to summer camp for two weeks.
We set up camp near the lake.

campaign.def: A planned series of actions to achieve a goal
ex: The company launched an advertising campaign.
She joined a campaign to protect the environment.

can.def: To be able to do something; to have permission
ex: I can swim.
Can you help me?
She can speak three languages.

cancel.def: To decide that something will not happen
ex: They cancelled the flight because of the storm.
I had to cancel my dentist appointment.
She gave a candid interview about her struggles.
Let me be candid with you — the results are not good.

capable.def: Having the ability to do something
ex: She's capable of handling difficult situations.
He's a very capable manager.

capricious.def: Given to sudden and unpredictable changes of mood or behavior
ex: The capricious weather ruined our outdoor plans repeatedly.
Working for a capricious boss made long-term planning impossible.

captain.def: The leader of a team or the person in charge of a ship
ex: He is the captain of the football team.
The captain welcomed the passengers on board.

car.def: A vehicle with four wheels and an engine
ex: He drives a red car.
I go to work by car.
She bought a new car.

card.def: A small piece of stiff paper for writing or playing games
ex: She sent me a birthday card.
Do you accept credit cards?
Please listen carefully to the instructions.
She carefully carried the glass of water.

carry.def: To hold something and move it to another place
ex: Can you carry this bag for me?
She always carries her phone.

cascade.def: A series of things that happen one after another in quick succession
ex: A cascade of errors led to the system failure.
The waterfall formed a beautiful cascade down the rocks.

cat.def: A small animal with fur that people keep as a pet
ex: The cat is sleeping on the sofa.
She has two cats.
I love cats.

category.def: A group of things that share similar qualities
ex: The books are organized by category.
This product falls into the luxury category.

cater.def: To provide food and drink or to satisfy a need or demand
ex: The hotel caters to business travelers.
The menu caters for vegetarians and vegans.
What was the cause of the fire?
Stress is a common cause of headaches.

cautious.def: Careful to avoid potential problems or dangers
ex: Investors remain cautious about the housing market.
She took a cautious approach to the negotiations.

ceiling.def: The top surface of a room
ex: There is a crack in the ceiling.
The ceiling in this room is very high.

celebrate.def: To do something special for a happy event
ex: We celebrated her birthday with a big party.
How do you celebrate New Year in your country?
This castle was built in the 15th century.
A lot has changed in the last century.

change.def: To become different; to make something different
ex: Things can change quickly.
I need to change my clothes.
She changed her mind.

character.def: A person in a book, film, or play
ex: Who is your favorite character in the movie?
The main character is a young doctor.
There is no charge for children under five.
What is the charge for delivery?

cheap.def: Not costing a lot of money
ex: This restaurant is cheap.
I found a cheap flight.
It looks cheap but it is good quality.

check.def: To look at something to see if it is correct or safe
ex: Please check your answers before submitting.
I need to check my email.

chest.def: The front part of your body between your neck and stomach
ex: He had a pain in his chest.
She held the baby close to her chest.

child.def: A young person who is not yet an adult
ex: She was a happy child.
Every child needs love.
The child is playing outside.

circle.def: A round shape like the letter O
ex: The children sat in a circle on the floor.
Draw a circle in the middle of the page.

city.def: A large town where many people live and work
ex: Istanbul is a big city.
I live in the city center.
The city is beautiful at night.

civic.def: Relating to the duties or activities of citizens
ex: Voting is a civic responsibility.
The city invested in civic infrastructure like parks and libraries.

claim.def: To say something is true without proving it
ex: He claims to speak five languages.
The company claims their product is the best.

clarity.def: The quality of being clear and easy to understand
ex: The report was praised for its clarity.
She spoke with remarkable clarity about complex issues.

class.def: A group of students who learn together; a lesson
ex: I have class at 9 am.
She is the best in her class.
English class is fun.

clean.def: Free from dirt; to remove dirt from something
ex: The house is very clean.
Please clean your room.
I need clean clothes.

climb.def: To go up something using your hands and feet
ex: They climbed the mountain in three hours.
The cat climbed up the tree.
Please close the door.
The shop is close to my house.
She closed her eyes.

clothes.def: Things that you wear on your body
ex: She bought new clothes.
Put on warm clothes.
I need to wash my clothes.

cloud.def: A white or grey thing in the sky made of tiny water drops
ex: Dark clouds appeared before the rain.
There is not a single cloud in the sky today.

cluster.def: A group of similar things or people positioned closely together
ex: A cluster of stars was visible in the night sky.
The disease appeared in clusters in certain neighborhoods.

coast.def: The land next to the sea
ex: We drove along the coast for hours.
The town is on the south coast.

coerce.def: To persuade someone to do something by using force or threats
ex: Witnesses claimed they had been coerced into signing confessions.
No one should be coerced into making medical decisions.

cognitive.def: Relating to mental processes of perception, memory, and reasoning
ex: Cognitive decline is common in old age.
The study examines the cognitive development of children.

cohesion.def: The action of forming a united whole
ex: Team cohesion improved significantly after the retreat.
The essay lacks cohesion — the paragraphs don't connect logically.

cold.def: Having a low temperature; not warm
ex: It is very cold today.
The water is cold.
I have a cold.

collaborate.def: To work jointly with others on an activity or project
ex: The two universities collaborated on groundbreaking cancer research.
Artists and engineers collaborated to create the interactive exhibit.
I'm going to lunch with some colleagues.
She's well-liked by all her colleagues.

collect.def: To bring things together or to get things as a hobby
ex: He collects stamps from different countries.
I will collect the children from school at three.

combine.def: To join two or more things together
ex: Combine the flour and sugar in a bowl.
She combines work with studying.

come.def: To move toward or arrive at a place
ex: Come here, please.
She came home late.
Can you come to my party?

comfortable.def: Making you feel relaxed and at ease
ex: This sofa is very comfortable.
Are you comfortable in that chair?
The ceremony will commence at noon.
Construction is set to commence next month.

commercial.def: Related to buying and selling things
ex: The building is used for commercial purposes.
The film was a huge commercial success.

commit.def: To promise to do something or to do something wrong
ex: He committed himself to finishing the project.
The crime was committed at night.

community.def: A group of people living in the same area or sharing interests
ex: The local community organized a festival.
She's very active in the online gaming community.

compare.def: To look at two things to see how they are different or similar
ex: Compare these two pictures and find the differences.
You cannot compare a city with a village.

compel.def: To force someone to do something
ex: The law compels employers to provide safety equipment.
She felt compelled to speak out against injustice.

compete.def: To try to win against others
ex: Five teams will compete in the final.
She competed in the swimming race.

complain.def: To say you are unhappy about something
ex: He always complains about the food.
If the service is bad, you should complain to the manager.

complement.def: To add to something in a way that enhances or improves it
ex: The wine complements the fish perfectly.
His skills complement hers — they make a great team.
She completed the course in six months.
Please complete this form and sign it.

complex.def: Having many different parts and difficult to understand
ex: The situation is more complex than it seems.
This is a very complex problem to solve.

compulsory.def: Required by law or a rule; obligatory
ex: Education is compulsory until age 16.
The company introduced compulsory drug testing.

computer.def: An electronic machine that stores and processes information
ex: I work on a computer all day.
She bought a new computer.

concede.def: To admit that something is true after first resisting it
ex: He conceded that his initial approach had been flawed.
The candidate conceded defeat shortly after midnight.

concern.def: A feeling of worry or something that worries you
ex: Safety is our main concern.
There is growing concern about pollution.

concise.def: Giving a lot of information clearly in a few words
ex: Please keep your answers concise and to the point.
The manual provides concise instructions for each step.

conclusion.def: The end or final decision about something
ex: In conclusion, I think we should accept the offer.
What conclusions did you reach?

condemn.def: To express strong disapproval of something
ex: World leaders condemned the terrorist attack.
The building was condemned as unsafe for habitation.

condition.def: The state that something or someone is in
ex: The car is in excellent condition.
He has a heart condition.

condone.def: To accept behavior that is considered morally wrong
ex: The university does not condone plagiarism in any form.
Silence in the face of injustice can be seen as condoning it.

conducive.def: Making a certain situation or outcome likely or possible
ex: A quiet environment is conducive to studying.
The warm climate is conducive to growing tropical fruit.
She is confident about passing the exam.
He became more confident after the training.

confirm.def: To say that something is definitely true or will happen
ex: Can you confirm your reservation?
The doctor confirmed that he was healthy.

conflict.def: A serious disagreement or a war between groups
ex: There is a conflict between the two departments.
The conflict lasted for several years.

confuse.def: To make someone unable to think clearly
ex: The instructions confused me completely.
People often confuse the two brothers.

congestion.def: Overcrowding or blockage, especially of traffic or in the body
ex: Traffic congestion is a major problem in big cities.
The new road aims to reduce congestion in the city center.

conjure.def: To bring an image or idea to mind; to make appear as if by magic
ex: The music conjured memories of her childhood summers.
He conjured a solution seemingly out of thin air.
Connect the printer to your computer.
This road connects the two villages.

consent.def: Permission or agreement for something to happen
ex: Patients must give their consent before surgery.
The contract was signed with the consent of both parties.

consequence.def: A result of an action, especially a bad one
ex: You must accept the consequences of your actions.
Losing his job was a consequence of his behavior.

considerable.def: Large enough to be noticeable or important
ex: She spent a considerable amount of money.
There has been considerable interest in the project.

consist.def: To be made up of particular parts
ex: The team consists of five members.
The exam consists of three sections.

consolidate.def: To make something stronger or more solid; to combine into one
ex: The company consolidated its position as market leader.
Several small departments were consolidated into a single division.

constant.def: Happening all the time without stopping
ex: There was a constant noise from the traffic.
She lives under constant pressure at work.
They're constructing a new bridge over the river.
The building was constructed in 1920.

consumer.def: A person who buys goods or services
ex: Consumers are demanding better quality products.
The law protects consumers from unfair prices.
Please contact us if you have any questions.
I'll contact you when I arrive.
This box contains old photos.
The drink contains a lot of sugar.

contempt.def: The feeling that something or someone is worthless or beneath consideration
ex: She looked at him with contempt.
He was held in contempt of court for refusing to testify.

contentious.def: Causing or likely to cause disagreement or argument
ex: Immigration remains a contentious political issue.
The contentious clause was eventually removed from the contract.

contingency.def: A future event or circumstance that is possible but cannot be predicted
ex: We should prepare a contingency plan in case of failure.
The budget includes funds for contingencies.

contradict.def: To say the opposite of what someone else has said; to be inconsistent with
ex: The witness contradicted his earlier statement.
The results contradict the original hypothesis.

contribute.def: To give something to help achieve a result
ex: Everyone contributed ideas to the project.
She contributes articles to a magazine.

control.def: To have power over something or make it do what you want
ex: She cannot control her anger sometimes.
The pilot controlled the plane during the storm.

convergence.def: The process of coming together from different directions to meet at a point
ex: There is a convergence of opinion among experts.
The convergence of technology and education is transforming classrooms.

conversation.def: A talk between two or more people
ex: We had a long conversation about our future.
She started a conversation with the man next to her.

convince.def: To make someone believe something or agree to do something
ex: I couldn't convince her to change her mind.
He convinced me that it was the right decision.

cook.def: To prepare food by heating it
ex: I like to cook dinner.
She cooks very well.
Who will cook tonight?
The weather is cool today.
That is a cool idea!
She thinks she is very cool.

cope.def: To deal with something difficult successfully
ex: How do you cope with stress at work?
She's finding it hard to cope with the changes.

corridor.def: A long passage in a building or a strip of land connecting areas
ex: She walked down the corridor to the director's office.
The country serves as a trade corridor between east and west.

corroborate.def: To confirm or give support to a statement or theory
ex: DNA evidence corroborated the witness's testimony.
No other source could corroborate his version of events.

counterpart.def: A person or thing that has the same function as another in a different place or system
ex: The foreign minister met with her American counterpart.
This species has no counterpart in the Southern Hemisphere.

country.def: A nation with its own government; rural area
ex: Turkey is a beautiful country.
Which country are you from?
I prefer the country to the city.

couple.def: Two people who are together, or a small number
ex: The couple sat together at the restaurant.
I will be ready in a couple of minutes.

course.def: A series of lessons; naturally, of course
ex: I am taking an English course.
Of course you can come!

crash.def: To hit something hard, causing damage
ex: The car crashed into a tree.
My computer crashed and I lost all my work.
That was a crazy idea but it worked!
He is crazy about football.
The witness gave a credible account of the incident.
There is no credible alternative to the current plan.
Crime is a serious problem in big cities.
He was sent to prison for his crimes.

crisis.def: A time of great difficulty or danger
ex: The country is facing an economic crisis.
We need a plan for dealing with a crisis.
This is a critical moment for the company.
She's very critical of other people's work.

critique.def: A detailed analysis and assessment of something
ex: She published a critique of modern education policy.
The professor offered a constructive critique of my essay.

crowd.def: A large group of people in one place
ex: A crowd gathered outside the stadium.
I don't like shopping in crowds.

cruel.def: Causing pain or suffering to others on purpose
ex: It is cruel to hurt animals.
The villain in the story is very cruel.

culminate.def: To reach a climax or the highest point
ex: Months of tension culminated in a full-scale strike.
The festival culminated in a spectacular fireworks display.

curb.def: To restrain or keep in check
ex: The government is trying to curb inflation.
He needs to curb his spending habits.

curious.def: Wanting to know or learn about something
ex: Children are naturally curious about the world.
I am curious about what happened.
What is your current address?
The current situation is very difficult.

curriculum.def: The subjects comprising a course of study in a school or college
ex: The national curriculum was revised last year.
Computer science is now part of the school curriculum.
Budget cuts forced the hospital to curtail its services.
Civil liberties were curtailed during the state of emergency.

cut.def: To use a knife or scissors to divide something
ex: She cut the bread.
I cut my finger.
Cut the paper in half.
My dad works in a hospital.
Dad, can I go out?
She looks like her dad.
The storm caused serious damage to the roof.
Smoking causes damage to your lungs.

dance.def: To move your body to music
ex: She loves to dance.
They danced all night.
Can you dance?

daughter.def: A female child of a parent
ex: She is my daughter.
Their daughter is 10 years old.

daunting.def: Seeming difficult to deal with; intimidating
ex: Starting a new business can be a daunting prospect.
The sheer volume of research material was daunting.

day.def: A period of 24 hours; the time when it is light
ex: What day is it today?
Have a nice day!
I work five days a week.

deadline.def: The latest time by which something must be done
ex: The deadline for applications is next Friday.
I always struggle to meet deadlines.
She has been deaf since she was a child.
He is deaf in one ear.

debate.def: A formal discussion about a topic
ex: There's been a lot of debate about education reform.
The candidates had a debate on television.

debilitate.def: To make someone very weak and infirm
ex: The disease gradually debilitated his immune system.
Years of conflict debilitated the country's economy.

debris.def: Scattered pieces of waste or remains of something destroyed
ex: Rescue teams searched through the debris for survivors.
The explosion sent debris flying in all directions.
Technology has changed a lot in the last decade.
She lived abroad for over a decade.

decide.def: To choose something after thinking about it
ex: I cannot decide what to eat.
She decided to stay home.
Have you decided yet?

decisive.def: Settling an issue quickly and effectively; able to make decisions quickly
ex: The battle proved to be the decisive moment of the war.
A good leader must be decisive under pressure.

decline.def: To become smaller or less, or to refuse politely
ex: Sales have declined by 15 percent.
She declined the invitation to dinner.

deem.def: To regard or consider in a specified way
ex: The committee deemed the proposal unacceptable.
Any student deemed to have cheated will be expelled.

deference.def: Polite respect shown toward someone, especially due to their authority
ex: She spoke with deference to the senior professor.
In deference to local customs, visitors removed their shoes.

degree.def: A qualification from a university or a unit of measurement
ex: She has a degree in economics.
It was 35 degrees outside today.

delay.def: A situation where something happens later than planned
ex: There was a two-hour delay at the airport.
Sorry for the delay in replying to your email.

delegate.def: To entrust a task or responsibility to another person
ex: A good manager knows how to delegate tasks effectively.
She delegated the project to her most experienced team member.
It was a deliberate attempt to mislead the public.
She spoke in a slow, deliberate manner.

deliver.def: To take something to a person or place
ex: They deliver pizza to your door.
The postman delivered a package this morning.
The workers demanded higher pay.
She demanded an explanation from him.
His calm demeanor hid a deep anxiety within.
The judge noted the defendant's composed demeanor throughout the trial.

demographic.def: Relating to the structure of populations
ex: Demographic changes are affecting the housing market.
The company analyzed the demographic profile of its customers.

demonstrate.def: To show or prove something clearly
ex: The results demonstrate that the method works.
Let me demonstrate how to use this tool.

denounce.def: To publicly declare something to be wrong or evil
ex: Human rights groups denounced the government's actions.
He was denounced as a traitor by his former allies.

deny.def: To say that something is not true
ex: He denied stealing the money.
She denied any involvement in the plan.

depend.def: To be affected by or decided by something
ex: The price depends on the size.
You can always depend on her for help.

depict.def: To represent or show something in a picture or with words
ex: The mural depicts scenes from the country's independence struggle.
The novel depicts life in rural Turkey during the 1950s.

deplete.def: To use up or reduce the supply of something
ex: Overfishing has severely depleted stocks in the North Sea.
The marathon depleted her energy reserves completely.

deploy.def: To move into position for use; to utilize effectively
ex: The army deployed troops along the border.
The company deployed new software across all departments.

designate.def: To officially assign a particular status or name to something
ex: The area was designated as a national park.
She was designated as the team leader for the project.

desire.def: A strong feeling of wanting something
ex: She has a strong desire to travel the world.
His desire for success drives him forward.
Despite the rain, we had a great time.
She passed the exam despite not studying much.

destroy.def: To damage something so badly that it cannot be used
ex: The fire destroyed the entire building.
War destroys people's lives.

detain.def: To keep someone in official custody
ex: Police detained three suspects for questioning.
He was detained at the border for several hours.

determine.def: To find out or decide something
ex: We need to determine the cause of the problem.
Your effort will determine your success.
Smoking is detrimental to your health.
The scandal was detrimental to the company's reputation.

develop.def: To grow or change into something bigger or better
ex: The town developed quickly over ten years.
She is developing her language skills.

deviate.def: To depart from an established course or norm
ex: The pilot was forced to deviate from the planned flight path.
She never deviated from her original research hypothesis.

diagnosis.def: The identification of the nature of an illness or problem
ex: Early diagnosis improves the chances of recovery.
The mechanic's diagnosis was that the engine needed replacing.

dictate.def: To state or order something with authority; to control or influence
ex: I won't let anyone dictate how I live my life.
Market forces dictate the price of goods.

difficult.def: Not easy; hard to do or understand
ex: This question is difficult.
Learning a new language is difficult.
It was a difficult day.
Do you have a digital camera?
We live in a digital world now.

diligent.def: Having or showing careful and persistent effort
ex: Diligent students often outperform naturally gifted ones.
Through diligent research, she uncovered new archival evidence.

dilute.def: To make a liquid thinner or weaker; to reduce the strength of something
ex: Dilute the juice with water before serving.
The proposal was diluted to gain wider support.

dinner.def: The main meal of the day, usually in the evening
ex: What is for dinner tonight?
We had dinner at a restaurant.
Dinner is ready!

diplomatic.def: Relating to diplomacy; tactful in dealing with people
ex: The two countries restored diplomatic relations.
She gave a diplomatic answer to avoid offending anyone.

disappear.def: To stop being visible or to stop existing
ex: The cat disappeared behind the wall.
Many old traditions are disappearing.

discard.def: To get rid of something as no longer useful or desirable
ex: Please discard any expired medications.
The theory was eventually discarded in favor of a new one.

discern.def: To perceive or recognize something clearly
ex: It was difficult to discern the truth from the rumors.
She could discern a pattern in the seemingly random data.

discourse.def: Written or spoken communication or debate on a particular topic
ex: Political discourse has become increasingly polarized.
The book contributes to the academic discourse on climate change.

discover.def: To find something for the first time
ex: Scientists discovered a new species of fish.
I discovered a great restaurant near my office.

discuss.def: To talk about something with someone
ex: We need to discuss this problem together.
They discussed the plan over lunch.

display.def: To show something so people can see it
ex: The museum displays artwork from the 19th century.
She displayed great courage during the emergency.

disrupt.def: To interrupt the normal course of an activity or process
ex: The strike disrupted train services across the country.
New technology is disrupting traditional business models.

disseminate.def: To spread information or knowledge widely
ex: Social media allows misinformation to disseminate rapidly.
The organization disseminates research findings to policymakers.

dissent.def: Disagreement with an official or widely held view
ex: The regime does not tolerate political dissent.
There were voices of dissent within the party.

distinction.def: A clear difference between two things
ex: There's an important distinction between the two words.
She graduated with distinction.

diverge.def: To separate and go in different directions; to differ
ex: At this point, the two accounts of events diverge significantly.
The path diverged into two trails at the edge of the forest.

do.def: To perform an action; to complete a task
ex: What do you do?
I do my homework after school.
She did a great job.

doctor.def: A person whose job is to treat sick people
ex: I need to see a doctor.
She is a doctor at the hospital.
The doctor said I am fine.

doctrine.def: A set of beliefs or principles held by a group or organization
ex: The party's economic doctrine favors free markets.
Military doctrine has evolved considerably since the Cold War.

dog.def: A common animal that people keep as a pet
ex: I have a big dog.
The dog is barking.
She walks her dog every morning.

donor.def: A person who donates something, especially money or blood
ex: The hospital urgently needs blood donors.
A generous donor funded the new research center.

door.def: A flat piece of wood or glass that you open to enter
ex: Please open the door.
Someone is at the door.
She closed the door quietly.
The volcano had been dormant for over 200 years before erupting.
The virus can remain dormant in the body for decades.

double.def: Twice as much or two of something
ex: I would like a double room, please.
The price has doubled in five years.

down.def: Toward a lower place; from high to low
ex: Sit down, please.
She walked down the stairs.
The sun is going down.

draft.def: A first version of a piece of writing
ex: I finished the first draft of my essay.
Can you review this draft before I submit it?

draw.def: To make a picture using a pen or pencil
ex: She likes to draw flowers.
Can you draw a map?
The child drew a house.

drink.def: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it
ex: I drink coffee every morning.
Would you like something to drink?
She drank all the water.

drive.def: To control and move a car or vehicle
ex: Can you drive?
She drives to work.
I learned to drive at 18.

drought.def: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall
ex: The drought caused widespread crop failure.
Parts of Africa suffer from severe drought every year.

due.def: Expected at a certain time or caused by
ex: The train is due at 3 o'clock.
The delay was due to bad weather.

during.def: Through the whole time of something
ex: It rained during the night.
She fell asleep during the movie.
I lived in London during 2020.

dust.def: Tiny pieces of dirt that you see on surfaces
ex: There was a thick layer of dust on the shelf.
She cleaned the dust off the table.

duty.def: Something you must do as part of your job or moral obligation
ex: It's your duty to report any problems.
The nurse was on duty all night.

dwell.def: To live in a place; to think or talk about something for a long time
ex: They dwell in a small village in the mountains.
There's no point dwelling on past mistakes.
She earns a good salary.
He earns money by teaching guitar.

economy.def: The system of money, trade, and industry in a country
ex: The economy is growing slowly this year.
Tourism is important for the local economy.

efficacy.def: The ability to produce a desired result
ex: Clinical trials demonstrated the efficacy of the new vaccine.
Researchers questioned the efficacy of the traditional teaching method.

election.def: A time when people vote to choose a leader
ex: The next election will be held in June.
She won the election by a large margin.

electronic.def: Using electricity and small parts to work
ex: I bought a new electronic device.
Electronic music is very popular now.

eloquent.def: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
ex: She delivered an eloquent speech that moved the audience to tears.
His silence was more eloquent than any words could be.
She was embarrassed when she forgot his name.
I felt embarrassed about my mistake.

embed.def: To fix firmly and deeply in a surrounding mass
ex: The journalist was embedded with the military unit.
These values are deeply embedded in our culture.

emergency.def: A serious situation that needs fast action
ex: Call 112 in an emergency.
The hospital is ready for any emergency.

emotion.def: A strong feeling such as love, anger, or fear
ex: He finds it hard to show his emotions.
Music can bring out strong emotions.
The school places great emphasis on teamwork.
There's too much emphasis on test results.

employ.def: To pay someone to work for you
ex: The company employs over 200 people.
She was employed as a teacher.
The room was completely empty.
Is this seat empty?
The internet enables people to work from home.
This key enables access to the building.

encompass.def: To include or contain a wide range of things
ex: The course encompasses everything from basic theory to advanced practice.
The national park encompasses over 500 square kilometers of wilderness.

encourage.def: To give someone hope or support to do something
ex: The teacher encouraged her students to read more.
His success encouraged me to try harder.

endorse.def: To declare public approval or support for something
ex: Several celebrities endorsed the charity campaign.
The committee endorsed the proposal unanimously.

endow.def: To provide with a quality or ability; to give money for a purpose
ex: Nature has endowed her with exceptional musical talent.
The billionaire endowed the university with a new research center.

engage.def: To become involved in an activity
ex: Students need to engage more in class discussions.
The company engages in international trade.

ensure.def: To make certain that something happens
ex: Please ensure all doors are locked before leaving.
We must ensure the safety of our customers.
I read the entire book in one day.
The entire class passed the exam.

envision.def: To imagine something as a future possibility
ex: She envisions a world without poverty.
The architect envisioned a modern, eco-friendly building.

epidemic.def: A widespread occurrence of a disease or undesirable phenomenon
ex: The country is facing an obesity epidemic.
The flu epidemic affected millions of people last winter.

erode.def: To gradually wear away or diminish
ex: Public trust in institutions has been eroded by repeated scandals.
Wind and rain eroded the cliff face over centuries.

erratic.def: Not even or regular in pattern; unpredictable
ex: His erratic behavior worried his colleagues.
The stock market has been erratic this month.

especially.def: More than usual or more than others
ex: I love all fruit, especially strawberries.
It is cold today, especially in the morning.

essence.def: The fundamental nature or most important quality of something
ex: The essence of democracy is freedom of choice.
In essence, the two proposals are very similar.

establish.def: To start or create something that will last
ex: The company was established in 1985.
They want to establish a good relationship with clients.

estimate.def: To guess the value or size of something
ex: I estimate the repairs will cost about 500 euros.
It's difficult to estimate how long it will take.

ethical.def: Relating to moral principles of right and wrong
ex: The experiment raised serious ethical questions.
Consumers are demanding more ethical business practices.

eventually.def: In the end, after a long time
ex: She eventually found her keys in her pocket.
He eventually agreed to help us.

evidence.def: Facts that prove something is true
ex: There is no evidence that he committed the crime.
The evidence shows that exercise improves health.

evoke.def: To bring a feeling, memory, or image to mind
ex: The music evoked memories of her childhood.
His speech evoked strong emotions in the audience.

exactly.def: In a way that is completely correct
ex: That is exactly what I mean.
The train left at exactly 9 o'clock.
The doctor examined his knee.
Police examined the car for evidence.
The food at that restaurant is excellent.
She did an excellent job on the project.

exert.def: To apply force, influence, or effort
ex: He exerted pressure on the committee to change the rules.
She had to exert herself to finish the marathon.

exile.def: The state of being forced to live away from one's own country
ex: The writer spent 20 years in exile.
The dictator was sent into exile after the revolution.

expand.def: To become or make something larger
ex: The company plans to expand into new markets.
The city has expanded rapidly in recent years.

expectation.def: A belief about what will happen
ex: The movie didn't meet my expectations.
There are high expectations for the new product.

expedite.def: To make a process happen more quickly
ex: We hired extra staff to expedite the processing of applications.
Digital systems can expedite communication between departments.
Travel expenses will be covered by the company.
Buying a house is a major expense.

explicit.def: Stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion
ex: The instructions were explicit about what to do.
She gave her explicit consent to the procedure.

exploit.def: To use something or someone unfairly for one's own advantage
ex: The company was accused of exploiting cheap labor overseas.
We need to exploit renewable energy sources more effectively.

explore.def: To travel around a new place to learn about it
ex: We explored the old part of the city.
Children love to explore nature.

expose.def: To make something visible or to put someone in a harmful situation
ex: The report exposed serious problems in the system.
Children should not be exposed to violence.

extend.def: To make something longer or bigger
ex: They extended the deadline by one week.
The road extends all the way to the coast.

extra.def: More than what is normal or expected
ex: Would you like extra cheese on your pizza?
She works extra hours on Fridays.
The heat was extreme last summer.
Extreme sports are dangerous but exciting.

fabricate.def: To invent or concoct something, typically to deceive
ex: He was accused of fabricating evidence.
The entire story was fabricated by the tabloid press.

faction.def: A small organized dissenting group within a larger one
ex: The party split into several rival factions.
The moderate faction pushed for compromise.

factor.def: One of the things that affects a situation
ex: Cost is an important factor in the decision.
Several factors contributed to the problem.

factory.def: A building where things are made by machines
ex: My father works in a car factory.
The factory produces 1000 units a day.

fail.def: To not succeed in doing something
ex: He failed his driving test twice.
The plan failed because of bad weather.

fairly.def: More than a little but not very
ex: The test was fairly easy.
She speaks English fairly well.

familiar.def: Known to you, easy to recognize
ex: His face looks familiar to me.
Are you familiar with this software?

fault.def: Responsibility for a mistake or problem
ex: It was my fault that we missed the bus.
The accident was not her fault.

feasible.def: Possible to do easily or conveniently
ex: The plan is technically feasible but expensive.
Is it feasible to complete the project by December?

feature.def: An important or noticeable part of something
ex: The phone has many useful features.
Her best feature is her sense of humor.
The female students organized the event.
There are more female doctors now than before.
She was a fervent advocate for human rights.
His fervent belief in justice drove him to become a lawyer.

fever.def: A body temperature that is higher than normal
ex: She stayed home because she had a fever.
The child had a high fever all night.

fierce.def: Having a violent or aggressive nature; very intense
ex: There is fierce competition for university places.
A fierce storm battered the coastline overnight.

fiscal.def: Relating to government revenue, especially taxes
ex: The government announced new fiscal policies.
The fiscal year ends in March.
The flight to Istanbul takes two hours.
Our flight was delayed by one hour.

flood.def: A large amount of water covering land that is usually dry
ex: The flood destroyed many homes.
Heavy rain caused floods in the south.

fluctuate.def: To rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
ex: Oil prices fluctuate according to global supply and demand.
Her mood tends to fluctuate dramatically throughout the day.
I will remember this moment forever.
Nothing lasts forever.

forge.def: To create something strong or successful; to shape metal by heating
ex: The two nations forged a strong alliance.
She forged a successful career in journalism.

former.def: Having been something in the past but not now
ex: The former president gave a speech.
She's a former colleague of mine.

forthcoming.def: About to happen or appear; willing to give information
ex: Details will be announced in the forthcoming weeks.
He was not very forthcoming about his past.

foster.def: To encourage the development of something
ex: The program aims to foster creativity in young students.
Good leadership fosters a culture of trust and innovation.

foundation.def: The base on which something is built
ex: Trust is the foundation of any good relationship.
The foundation of the building is very strong.

fragment.def: A small part broken off or separated from something
ex: Fragments of pottery were found at the site.
I only caught a fragment of their conversation.

freedom.def: The right to do what you want without being controlled
ex: Freedom of speech is a basic human right.
She enjoys the freedom of working from home.

freeze.def: To become very cold and turn to ice
ex: Water freezes at zero degrees.
I am freezing — can you close the window?
Buses are frequent during rush hour.
She makes frequent trips to London.
I always buy fresh vegetables from the market.
Let me get you some fresh towels.

friction.def: Conflict or disagreement between people; resistance when surfaces rub
ex: There is growing friction between the two departments.
Friction between the tire and road helps the car stop.

fulfil.def: To achieve or realize something desired or promised
ex: She finally fulfilled her dream of becoming a pilot.
The company failed to fulfil its contractual obligations.

fund.def: Money saved or collected for a particular purpose
ex: They set up a fund to help disaster victims.
The government cut funds for education.

furniture.def: Tables, chairs, beds, etc. in a room
ex: We need to buy new furniture for the living room.
The furniture in this office is very old.
All attempts to negotiate a ceasefire proved futile.
It is futile to argue with someone who refuses to listen to evidence.

gain.def: To get or achieve something useful
ex: She gained a lot of experience in her first job.
The team gained confidence after winning.

gap.def: A space between two things or a difference
ex: There's a big gap between the rich and the poor.
He returned after a gap of five years.

garage.def: A building where you keep your car
ex: He parked his car in the garage.
The garage door was left open.

gather.def: To come together or collect things
ex: People gathered in the square to watch the show.
She gathered her belongings and left.

generation.def: All the people born around the same time
ex: The younger generation is more comfortable with technology.
This tradition has been passed down for generations.

gentle.def: Kind, calm, and careful not to hurt
ex: Be gentle with the baby.
She has a gentle voice.

glimpse.def: A momentary or partial view of something
ex: I caught a glimpse of the celebrity as she left the hotel.
The documentary offers a glimpse into life behind bars.
My goal is to speak English fluently.
He scored three goals in one match.

grasp.def: To seize and hold firmly; to understand something
ex: It took me a while to grasp the concept.
She grasped his hand tightly and refused to let go.
I'm grateful for all your support.
She was grateful to have such good friends.

grievance.def: A real or imagined cause for complaint
ex: Workers filed a grievance against the management.
The treaty failed to address their long-standing grievances.
The athletes endured a grueling training schedule for months.
After a grueling 14-hour flight, we finally arrived.

guarantee.def: To promise that something will happen
ex: I can't guarantee that the weather will be nice.
The product is guaranteed for two years.

guest.def: A person you invite to your home or to an event
ex: We are having guests for dinner tonight.
The hotel has 200 guest rooms.

guilty.def: Having done something wrong, or feeling bad about it
ex: The jury found him guilty.
I feel guilty about not calling my mother.

habit.def: Something you do regularly, often without thinking
ex: Smoking is a bad habit.
She has a habit of biting her nails.

halt.def: To bring or come to a sudden stop
ex: Production was halted due to safety concerns.
The train halted at a signal just outside the station.

hamper.def: To prevent the free movement or progress of something
ex: Heavy rain hampered rescue efforts in the flooded region.
Lack of funding continues to hamper scientific research.

handle.def: To deal with a situation or problem
ex: She handled the problem very well.
I cannot handle all this work alone.

hang.def: To put something so it is held from above
ex: She hung the picture on the wall.
Hang your coat on the hook.
I can hardly hear you — speak louder.
She hardly ever goes out at night.

harness.def: To control and make use of natural resources or energy
ex: Scientists are finding new ways to harness solar energy.
She learned to harness her anger and use it productively.
The winter weather was very harsh this year.
His criticism was harsh but fair.

hazard.def: A danger or risk to health or safety
ex: Smoking is a serious health hazard.
The icy roads pose a hazard to drivers.
There was a heap of dirty clothes on the floor.
He left a heap of papers on his desk.

heritage.def: Valued objects and qualities such as buildings and traditions passed down through generations
ex: The city is proud of its rich cultural heritage.
UNESCO works to protect world heritage sites.

hesitate.def: To pause before doing something because you are unsure
ex: Don't hesitate to ask if you need help.
She hesitated before answering the question.

hierarchy.def: A system in which people or things are arranged according to importance
ex: He quickly rose through the corporate hierarchy.
There is a clear hierarchy of needs in psychology.

highlight.def: To emphasize or draw attention to something
ex: The report highlights the need for more funding.
She highlighted the key points in her presentation.

hinder.def: To make it difficult for something to happen or develop
ex: Bureaucratic delays hindered the delivery of humanitarian aid.
Poor infrastructure hinders economic growth in rural areas.

hostile.def: Showing strong dislike or opposition; unfriendly
ex: The crowd was openly hostile toward the politician.
They faced a hostile business environment abroad.
They live in a huge house.
The concert was a huge success.

humanitarian.def: Concerned with reducing suffering and improving conditions for people
ex: The organization provides humanitarian aid to refugees.
The conflict created a humanitarian crisis.

hypocrisy.def: The practice of claiming standards that one does not actually follow
ex: Critics accused the government of hypocrisy on environmental policy.
The hypocrisy of preaching tolerance while practicing discrimination was obvious.
The police are trying to discover the identity of the thief.
Living abroad can change your sense of identity.

illusion.def: A false idea or belief; something that seems real but is not
ex: Freedom of choice is sometimes just an illusion.
The magician created the illusion of floating in air.
Please call me back immediately.
She immediately knew something was wrong.

immense.def: Extremely large or great in size or degree
ex: The project required an immense amount of effort.
She felt immense relief when the results came back negative.
Alcohol can impair your ability to drive.
The disease progressively impairs cognitive function.

impede.def: To delay or prevent something by obstructing it
ex: Thick fog impeded visibility on the motorway.
Legal challenges continue to impede the construction project.
It is imperative that we act now.
Reducing carbon emissions is an environmental imperative.

implication.def: A likely consequence of something; something suggested without being stated directly
ex: The financial implications of the decision are enormous.
The implication was that he had been lying.

impress.def: To make someone admire or respect you
ex: She impressed everyone with her presentation.
I was impressed by the quality of his work.
I want to improve my English.
The weather is improving slowly.

inadvertent.def: Not resulting from deliberate planning; unintentional
ex: The leak was the result of an inadvertent error by a technician.
His inadvertent remark offended several colleagues.

inclination.def: A tendency or preference toward a particular action or state
ex: She showed no inclination to compromise on the issue.
His natural inclination was toward solitude rather than social gatherings.

incur.def: To become subject to something unpleasant as a result of one's actions
ex: The company incurred heavy losses last quarter.
You may incur additional charges for late payment.

indeed.def: Used to make something stronger or confirm it
ex: It is indeed a difficult problem.
Thank you very much indeed.

indicate.def: To show or point to something
ex: Research indicates that sleep is essential for health.
The sign indicates the way to the exit.

indifferent.def: Having no particular interest or sympathy; unconcerned
ex: The public seemed indifferent to the plight of refugees.
She remained indifferent to both praise and criticism.

indigenous.def: Originating or occurring naturally in a particular place
ex: The indigenous population has lived here for thousands of years.
Several indigenous plant species are threatened by development.
Clean water is indispensable for human survival.
She quickly became an indispensable member of the research team.

induce.def: To bring about or cause; to persuade someone to do something
ex: The drug can induce drowsiness.
Nothing could induce him to change his mind.

infer.def: To deduce from evidence and reasoning rather than explicit statement
ex: From the data, we can infer that the treatment is effective.
What can we infer about the author's intentions from this passage?

inflict.def: To cause something unpleasant to be suffered by someone
ex: The hurricane inflicted severe damage on coastal towns.
He inflicted years of suffering on his victims.
My initial reaction was surprise.
The initial cost is high, but it saves money later.

insist.def: To say firmly that something must happen
ex: She insisted on paying for dinner.
He insists that he is innocent.

inspire.def: To give someone the desire to do something
ex: Her story inspired me to follow my dreams.
The teacher inspired his students to love reading.

instructions.def: Information telling you how to do something
ex: Read the instructions before using the machine.
Follow the instructions on the packet.

insurance.def: An agreement where a company pays costs if something bad happens
ex: Do you have travel insurance?
Car insurance is required by law.

insurgent.def: A person fighting against a government or authority within their own country
ex: Government forces clashed with insurgents near the border.
The insurgent movement gained popular support in the countryside.

integrity.def: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles; completeness
ex: She is a woman of great personal integrity.
The structural integrity of the bridge was compromised.

intend.def: To plan or mean to do something
ex: I intend to finish this book today.
She intended to call but forgot.

interim.def: Temporary; in or for the intervening period
ex: An interim government was formed until elections could be held.
In the interim, please continue with your current duties.

investigate.def: To try to find out the truth about something
ex: Police are investigating the robbery.
We need to investigate this matter further.

invite.def: To ask someone to come to an event or place
ex: She invited all her friends to the party.
Would you like to invite him for dinner?

invoke.def: To cite or appeal to someone or something as an authority; to call on
ex: The president invoked emergency powers.
She invoked the memory of past leaders in her speech.
Climate change is a global issue.
We need to address this issue immediately.

journey.def: The act of traveling from one place to another
ex: The journey took five hours by train.
Have a safe journey!

jurisdiction.def: The official power to make legal decisions; the area over which authority extends
ex: The case falls outside our jurisdiction.
Each state has jurisdiction over its own criminal law.

lament.def: To express passionate grief or regret about something
ex: Teachers lament the declining quality of student writing.
She lamented the loss of traditional crafts in modern society.

lawyer.def: A person whose job is to help people with the law
ex: She hired a lawyer to help with the case.
He wants to become a lawyer.

lazy.def: Not wanting to work or be active
ex: He is too lazy to clean his room.
I felt lazy and stayed in bed all morning.

lend.def: To give something to someone for a short time
ex: Can you lend me some money until Friday?
She lent her umbrella to her colleague.

lethal.def: Sufficient to cause death; extremely dangerous
ex: The venom is lethal to humans within hours if untreated.
A lethal combination of speed and poor visibility caused the crash.

levy.def: To impose a tax, fee, or fine
ex: The government plans to levy a new tax on imports.
A fine was levied against the company for pollution.

liable.def: Legally responsible; likely to do something
ex: The manufacturer is liable for any defects in the product.
If you don't fix the roof, it's liable to collapse.

limit.def: The most that is allowed or possible
ex: The speed limit here is 50 km/h.
There is a limit to what I can do.

linger.def: To remain in a place longer than necessary; to persist
ex: The smell of smoke lingered in the building for days.
Doubts about his guilt lingered long after the trial ended.
